features.name: Hit Triage Workflow
features.description: Application for loading compound sets into campaigns, applying configurable scoring functions, and deciding which hits advance to the next stage.
features.evidence_summary: All 'hitTriage'-prefixed functions cluster with the 'Hit Triage' app entry point, role-tagged demo data sources, a submit function, and the dedicated developer guide for building triage systems.

features.name: Hit Design Workflow
features.description: Application for designing and registering new compounds within an active campaign, with per-molecule VID tracking and integration with compute functions.
features.evidence_summary: All three members share the 'Hit Design' name prefix; the V-iD panel is the designer's molecule-registry UI surface, registered as a panel for Hit Design.

features.name: Peptide Triage Workflows
features.description: Peptide-focused variants of the triage framework — PeptiHit for hit triage and PepTriage for broader peptide campaign management.
features.evidence_summary: Four functions share 'Pepti'/'Pep' naming with paired app+treeBrowser entries plus a peptide demo data source; changelog 1.10.0 explicitly introduced PepTriage as a peptide-focused workflow.

features.name: Molecule Registry (VID System)
features.description: A global virtual-ID registry that assigns canonical VIDs to molecules and tracks their presence across multiple campaigns.
features.evidence_summary: The registration function and all five SQL queries directly reference VID operations (add, get, register); changelog 1.9.0 introduced the 'Global VID system and molecule tracking across multiple campaigns'.

features.name: Campaign Management
features.description: Infrastructure for creating, organizing, locking, and collaborating on campaigns — covering grouping, sorting, column visibility, file storage, and multi-user change signaling.
features.evidence_summary: Lock/release/modified queries implement the collaboration locking layer; the package settings editor governs campaign defaults; changelog entries across 1.4.3–1.10.0 confirm grouping, sorting, file storage, and multi-user signaling as a distinct management concern.

features.name: Gasteiger Charge Cell Renderer
features.description: A custom grid cell renderer that visualizes Gasteiger partial charges on molecule structures within campaign tables.
features.evidence_summary: Sole cellRenderer function in the package, named after the Gasteiger charge algorithm and registered with role='cellRenderer'.
